📖 Glossary

ApterygotaIntroduction to Entomology
Primitive, wingless insects with no metamorphosis.
ArthropodsIntroduction to Entomology
Invertebrates with a hard exoskeleton and jointed appendages; the largest animal phylum.
CulicidaeMosquitoes
The mosquito family, within Order Diptera.
cuticleIntroduction to Entomology
The hard, protective exoskeleton covering an arthropod's body.
ecdysisIntroduction to Entomology
The periodic shedding of an insect's old exoskeleton, also called moulting.
ecdysoneIntroduction to Entomology
The moulting hormone that triggers ecdysis.
endophagicMosquitoes
Feeding indoors.
endophilicMosquitoes
Resting indoors after feeding.
EntomologyIntroduction to Entomology
The scientific study of insects and other arthropods.
euryhalineAnopheline & Culicine Mosquitoes
Capable of tolerating a wide range of salinities — able to breed in brackish or saline water.
exophagicMosquitoes
Feeding outdoors.
exophilicMosquitoes
Resting outdoors after feeding.
gonotrophic cycleMosquitoes
The repeated cycle of blood feeding, egg maturation, and oviposition in female mosquitoes.
haemocoelIntroduction to Entomology
The body cavity of an arthropod, filled with haemolymph, functioning as an open circulatory system.
haemolymphIntroduction to Entomology
The colourless fluid filling the insect body cavity, performing the combined roles of blood and lymph.
harmonic convergenceMosquitoes
Courtship behaviour where a male mosquito modulates his wingbeat frequency to match the female's.
hemielytraLice & Bed Bugs
Vestigial, non-functional wings in bed bugs, lying over the meso- and metathorax.
holometabolousIntroduction to Entomology
Undergoing complete metamorphosis with a pupal stage: egg, larva, pupa, adult.
IVMAnopheline & Culicine Mosquitoes
Integrated Vector Management — the WHO-endorsed framework combining multiple control approaches to reduce vector-borne disease transmission.
juvenile hormoneIntroduction to Entomology
A hormone that regulates insect growth and prevents premature metamorphosis.
Malpighian tubulesIntroduction to Entomology
Blind-ending tubes at the mid/hindgut junction that extract nitrogenous waste from the haemolymph.
Microfilarial periodicityAnopheline & Culicine Mosquitoes
The rhythmic 24-hour fluctuation in the number of microfilariae detectable in peripheral blood.
NitsLice & Bed Bugs
Louse eggs — oval, white, with a small lid (operculum), firmly cemented to hair shafts or clothing fibres.
palmate hairsAnopheline & Culicine Mosquitoes
Float hairs found on segments 1-7 of the Anopheles larval abdomen.
paroxysmsAnopheline & Culicine Mosquitoes
The classical malaria symptom sequence of cold, hot, and sweating stages, corresponding to synchronous rupture of infected red blood cells.
PediculosisLice & Bed Bugs
Infestation of the body, head hair, or pubic hair by lice.
peritrophic membraneIntroduction to Entomology
A protective lining of the midgut that prevents abrasion by food particles.
piloseMosquitoes
Sparsely haired — describes female mosquito antennae.
PlumoseMosquitoes
Densely feathery/bushy — describes male mosquito antennae.
proboscisMosquitoes
The mosquito's tube-like mouthpart, a bundle of six needle-like stylets enclosed in the labium.
PterygotaIntroduction to Entomology
Winged insects, further split by how their metamorphosis proceeds.
spermathecaIntroduction to Entomology
A muscular pouch in female insects that receives and stores sperm, enabling fertilization over extended periods from a single mating.
stylet sacLice & Bed Bugs
A pouch containing the louse's piercing "stabbing apparatus."
tracheal systemIntroduction to Entomology
The insect respiratory system: a network of internal air tubes delivering oxygen directly to tissues.

No terms match your search.
